paper_title: Data on elemental concentrations in marine sediments from the South and South West of England

methods.description: Process for collecting and validating elemental concentration data from marine sediments in Southern England, using data from the Environment Agency (EA) and the Marine Environment Monitoring and Assessment National (MERMAN) database.

steps.description: Data were obtained from two UK key public repositories - the Environment Agency (EA), and the Marine Environment Monitoring and Assessment National (MERMAN) database managed by the British Oceanographic Data Centre (BODC).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch data from EA and MERMAN databases

steps.description: Data were subjected to a rigorous selection-validation process.
Georeferenced EA sites were projected on to maps (Google Earth), after coordinate transformation from OSGB36 format to WGS84 for ease of use with Google Earth and ArcGis.
Multiple close coordinates for the same location were averaged so that each site only corresponded to a unique geolocation.
steps.searchable_summary: average multiple coordinates for unique geolocation

steps.description: Selected EA data with a 'MATERIAL_DESC' related to sediments and additional data with material identifiers not linked to sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: select data based on material description

methods.description: Organizing and presenting the collected data for end-user accessibility and interpretation.

steps.description: The secondary data (.csv extension file) have been organized and labelled for interrogation and searching by end-users.
steps.searchable_summary: organize and label data for user access

steps.description: The geo-spatial information contained in geographic data files (.kml extension files) enables end-users to directly visualize and select sites of interest from their geolocalisation on Google Earth.
steps.searchable_summary: create KML files for geospatial visualization

steps.description: Summary statistics are presented in Tables 1 and 2.
